test_cases.test_case: Successful login with required fields filled and 'Remember Me' checked redirects to Dashboard
preconditions: Unauthenticated user on Login page
steps: 1. Verify the Login button is disabled
2. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
3. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
4. Check the Remember Me checkbox
5. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: redirects to Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Clicking 'Forgot Password?' navigates to Forgot Password flow
preconditions: Unauthenticated user on Login page
steps: 1. Click the 'Forgot Password?' link below the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to Forgot Password flow

test_cases.test_case: Tenant dropdown shows default and allows selecting a different tenant
preconditions: Unauthenticated user on Login page
steps: 1. Observe the Tenant dropdown displays 'default' by default
2. Open the Tenant dropdown
3. Select <tenant> from the Tenant dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Tenant dropdown displays the selected <tenant>

test_cases.test_case: Language selector is visible and can change the selected language
preconditions: Unauthenticated user on Login page
steps: 1. Verify the Language selector is visible in the authentication panel
2. Open the Language selector
3. Select <language> from the Language selector
test_cases.expected_result: Language selector displays the selected <language>

test_cases.test_case: Username required — leave Username blank and attempt to submit
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Enter <valid password> into the Password field
3. Attempt to click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login button remains disabled; the Username field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; the form does not submit and the user stays on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Password required — leave Password blank and attempt to submit
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> into the Username field
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Attempt to click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login button remains disabled; the Password field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; the form does not submit and the user stays on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: All required fields empty — attempt to submit with both Username and Password blank
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Attempt to click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login button remains disabled; both Username and Password fields display inline validation errors indicating they are required; the form does not submit and the user stays on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Invalid credentials — submit with incorrect Username or Password
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter <invalid username> into the Username field
2. Enter <invalid password> into the Password field
3. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login attempt is blocked; page displays the error message 'Invalid credentials' and the user remains on the Login page (no redirect to Dashboard)

test_cases.test_case: Very long Username input handling (>200 chars)
preconditions: Login page is opened
Login form is visible
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter <maximum length string> (very long string 200+ characters) into the Username field
3. Enter a non-empty value into the Password field
4. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Username input accepts the <maximum length string> without client-side truncation and the Login button enables; after submit the form shows the server failure state (the 'Invalid credentials' error is shown).

test_cases.test_case: Username and Password with emoji / special unicode characters
preconditions: Login page is opened
Login form is visible
steps: 1. Enter a string containing emoji and extended Unicode characters into the Username field
2. Enter a string containing emoji and extended Unicode characters into the Password field
3. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Both fields accept emoji and extended Unicode characters (no immediate client-side error) and the Login button enables; after submit the UI shows a single server response — the 'Invalid credentials' error is shown (submission is blocked by authentication failure).

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in Username is trimmed or normalized
preconditions: Login page is opened
Login form is visible
steps: 1. Enter a Username value with leading and trailing spaces into the Username field
2. Move focus out of the Username field (blur)
3. Enter a non-empty value into the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: Whitespace trimming succeeds or normalization is applied: after blur the Username field's visible value shows no leading/trailing spaces (trimmed) and the Login button enables when Password is non-empty; subsequent submit either proceeds or shows 'Invalid credentials' depending on server — the visible trimming behavior must be present.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Login button prevents duplicate submissions
preconditions: Login page is opened
Login form is visible
Username and Password fields contain non-empty values
steps: 1. Ensure Username is filled with a non-empty value
2. Ensure Password is filled with a non-empty value
3. Rapidly double-click the Login button (two clicks in quick succession)
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission is blocked: only one submission is processed by the UI and only one server response is reflected in the UI — a single visible outcome appears (e.g. single 'Invalid credentials' error is shown) and no duplicate alerts/redirects are produced.

test_cases.test_case: Search Activity filters recent system activities
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Home page
2. Enter <search term> in the Search Activity input field
3. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Recent system activities list displays only activities matching <search term>; unrelated activities are no longer visible (filters recent system activities)

test_cases.test_case: Dashboard button navigates to Dashboard view
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Home page
2. Click the Dashboard button
test_cases.expected_result: Application navigates to the Dashboard page and the page title or header 'Dashboard' is visible (navigates to Dashboard)

test_cases.test_case: Home page displays welcome message and system version information
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Home page
2. Observe the main content area and page footer
test_cases.expected_result: Welcome card displays the message "Welcome, mifos!"; Mifos and Fineract system version information is visible in the page footer

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot load Home page
preconditions: User is not logged in
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, navigate to the Home Page URL
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: the user is redirected to the Login page; Home page content is not displayed (the welcome card 'Welcome, mifos!' and the Search Activity input are not visible).

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access Dashboard action
preconditions: User is not logged in
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, attempt to open the Dashboard view (navigate directly to the Dashboard URL or invoke the Dashboard action)
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked: the user is redirected to the Login page; the Dashboard view is not displayed and no navigation to Dashboard occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Very long search string (200+ characters) in Search Activity
preconditions: User is logged in
Home Page is displayed with Search Activity input visible
steps: 1. Focus the Search Activity input
2. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into the Search Activity input, including no special control characters
3. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Search submits successfully; the activity list updates (may show results or no results) and the UI remains responsive. The Search Activity input displays the entered long string (not silently truncated) or shows a visible truncation indicator if the UI intentionally truncates input. (Pass = search succeeds or shows a clear truncation indicator.)

test_cases.test_case: Search Activity with special characters, emoji, and leading/trailing whitespace
preconditions: User is logged in
Home Page is displayed with Search Activity input visible
steps: 1. Focus the Search Activity input
2. Enter a search string that includes special characters and emoji and has leading and trailing whitespace
3. Click the Search button
test_cases.expected_result: Search submits successfully; the activity list updates (may show results or no results). Leading/trailing whitespace is either trimmed in the visible Search Activity input after submission or a visible result shows the trimmed value. Special characters and emoji are preserved in the displayed search term or an inline error is shown if such characters are unsupported (in which case an error is shown). The test is considered passing if the search operation succeeds with a visible, deterministic handling of whitespace and special characters.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Search button
preconditions: User is logged in
Home Page is displayed with Search Activity input visible
Search Activity input has at least one character
steps: 1. Enter a non-empty value into the Search Activity input
2. Rapidly double-click the Search button (two clicks in quick succession)
test_cases.expected_result: Only one search operation is applied and the activity list updates once; no duplicate entries or repeated UI side-effects appear. The double-click does not create duplicate results or multiple visible filter applications — the search succeeds once.

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Dashboard from Home
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
Home page is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' button on the Home page
test_cases.expected_result: Dashboard page is displayed with the 'Search Activity' field visible at the top

test_cases.test_case: Client Trends chart displays when an office is selected
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
Home page is open
Office <selected office> is selected and has client data
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' button on the Home page
2. Observe the main chart area
test_cases.expected_result: Client Trends Chart is visible for the selected office and shows legend items 'New Clients' and 'Closed Clients'

test_cases.test_case: Summary cards show amounts when data exists for the selected office
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
Home page is open
Office <selected office> is selected and has dashboard financial data
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' button on the Home page
2. Observe the summary cards below the chart
test_cases.expected_result: Summary cards display Amount Pending / Disbursed and Amount Collected values for the selected office (numeric values visible on each card)

test_cases.test_case: Summary cards show 'No Data' when no information is available for the selected office
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
Home page is open
Office <selected office> is selected and has no dashboard data
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' button on the Home page
2. Observe the summary cards area below the chart
test_cases.expected_result: Both summary cards display 'No Data' for Amount Pending / Disbursed and Amount Collected

test_cases.test_case: Search Activity filters activity results on the Dashboard
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
Home page is open
Dashboard contains activity items for the selected office
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' button on the Home page
2. Enter <search term> in the 'Search Activity' field and submit the search
test_cases.expected_result: Activity list displays only items matching <search term>; unrelated activity items are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: View Client Trends chart without selecting an office (precondition not met)
preconditions: User is on the Home page
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' button on the Home page
2. Ensure no office is selected in the office selector (leave office selection empty/default)
3. Observe the Client Trends chart area
test_cases.expected_result: Client Trends Chart does not render because the precondition 'office must be selected' is not met; the chart area displays a visible prompt/instruction that an office must be selected and the chart data and legend items ('New Clients', 'Closed Clients') are not shown

test_cases.test_case: Summary cards show 'No Data' when selected office has no information
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' button on the Home page
2. Select <office_with_no_information> from the office selector
3. Observe the 'Amount Pending / Disbursed' and 'Amount Collected' summary cards
test_cases.expected_result: Amount Pending / Disbursed card displays a visible 'No Data' indicator for the selected office and does not show a numeric amount; Amount Collected card displays a visible 'No Data' indicator and does not show a numeric amount

test_cases.test_case: Open Dashboard without any office selected
preconditions: User is logged in
No office is selected in the application (global office selector is empty)
steps: 1. From the Home page, click the Dashboard link/button
2. Observe the Dashboard page without selecting an office
test_cases.expected_result: Viewing client trends is blocked until an office is selected: the Client Trends Chart area does not render chart data and the summary cards display 'No Data' for the selected office; the dashboard does not show client trend lines — is blocked / error shown

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly switch selected office repeatedly
preconditions: User is logged in
At least two offices exist: one with activity data and one with no activity data
steps: 1. From any page, open the office selector and select Office A (office with activity data)
2. Immediately switch the office selector to Office B (office with no activity data)
3. Immediately switch back to Office A
4. Stop switching and wait for dashboard to finish loading
test_cases.expected_result: Dashboard updates complete and the final selected office's data is shown: Client Trends Chart and summary cards reflect the final selection (if final office has data the chart and summary cards show data; if final office has no data the summary cards show 'No Data'); intermediate rapid switches do not leave stale data visible and the final update succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Double-click the Dashboard link on Home page
preconditions: User is on the Home page
No navigation has been initiated
steps: 1. Double-click the Dashboard button/link rapidly (two clicks within ~200ms)
test_cases.expected_result: Only one navigation to the Dashboard page occurs and only one Dashboard view is shown; no duplicate navigations or duplicated UI components appear — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Search with a very long search string (>200 characters)
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page
An office is selected (so search can query data)
steps: 1. Focus the Search Activity field
2. Enter a very long string (very long text >200 characters) into the Search Activity field
3. Press Enter or click the search action
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes without UI crash and the dashboard remains responsive; either search completes and displays matching results or returns a visible 'No results' state; the search action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Search containing leading and trailing whitespace
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page
An office is selected
steps: 1. Focus the Search Activity field
2. Enter a search term with leading and trailing whitespace (e.g., '  term  ')
3. Press Enter or click the search action
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes successfully; leading and trailing whitespace are trimmed in the effective search term (visible by results that match the trimmed term) and the dashboard displays results for the trimmed query or a visible 'No results' message — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Search with special characters and emoji
preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page
An office is selected
steps: 1. Focus the Search Activity field
2. Enter a string containing special characters and emoji into the Search Activity field
3. Press Enter or click the search action
test_cases.expected_result: Search processes the input without UI errors: either the search returns matching results or a visible 'No results' message; the dashboard remains responsive and no unhandled error is shown — action succeeds

test_cases.test_case: Live search displays grouped results with name, identifier and status
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter <partial query> in the Query field (live search)
3. Observe the results dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Results dropdown is displayed and is grouped by 'Clients', 'Groups', 'Loans', and 'Savings accounts'; each visible result shows an Entity Name, Identifier and Status

test_cases.test_case: Search supports partial matching (results contain the entered partial term)
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter <partial term> in the Query field
3. Observe the results dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Results dropdown displays items whose names contain <partial term> (partial matching is applied); unrelated items are not shown

test_cases.test_case: Search is case-insensitive (mixed-case query returns matches)
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter <mixed-case term> in the Query field
3. Observe the results dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Results dropdown displays matches for <mixed-case term> regardless of letter case (case-insensitive matching)

test_cases.test_case: Selecting a Client result opens the Client detail page
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter <client search term> in the Query field so Client results are shown
3. Click a result under the 'Clients' group
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to Client detail page

test_cases.test_case: Selecting a Group result opens the Group detail page
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter <group search term> in the Query field so Group results are shown
3. Click a result under the 'Groups' group
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to Group detail page

test_cases.test_case: Selecting a Loan result opens the Loan detail page
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter <loan search term> in the Query field so Loan results are shown
3. Click a result under the 'Loans' group
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to Loan detail page

test_cases.test_case: Selecting a Savings account result opens the Savings account detail page
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter <savings search term> in the Query field so Savings accounts results are shown
3. Click a result under the 'Savings accounts' group
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to Savings account detail page

test_cases.test_case: Empty-state shows 'No results found' when there are no matches
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter <non-matching term> in the Query field
3. Observe the results dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: The results dropdown displays the message 'No results found'

test_cases.test_case: Results dropdown visibility follows Query != '' condition
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter <term> in the Query field and observe the results dropdown is visible
3. Clear the Query field and observe the results dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Results dropdown is displayed while Query is non-empty; after clearing Query the results dropdown is no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user attempts to open Global Search via toolbar Search Icon
preconditions: User is logged out / unauthenticated
steps: 1. Open the application home page while not logged in
2. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar
test_cases.expected_result: Global Search is not opened; the user is redirected to the login page or a login modal is shown; the Global Search input field is not visible

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user navigates directly to Global Search page/URL
preconditions: User is logged out / unauthenticated
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged out
2. Enter <Global Search direct URL> in the browser address bar and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page and the Global Search input is not displayed; Global Search content is not accessible prior to login

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to select a search result when Query is blank (results dropdown visibility precondition violated)
preconditions: User is logged in
steps: 1. Log in as a valid user
2. Ensure the Global Search Query field is empty
3. Attempt to click on a result row or result area in the top toolbar where results would normally appear
test_cases.expected_result: Results dropdown is not visible (since Query = ''); no result rows are clickable; no navigation to any detail page occurs

test_cases.test_case: Type a query that yields no matches and attempt to select the 'No results found' message
preconditions: User is logged in
steps: 1. Log in as a valid user
2. Click the Search Icon to open Global Search
3. Enter <query with no matches> into the Query field
4. Wait for live search to complete and the results dropdown to appear
5. Click the 'No results found' message in the dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: The results dropdown displays the 'No results found' message; clicking the message does not navigate to any detail page and no entity is opened; the search action is blocked

test_cases.test_case: Empty query hides results dropdown
preconditions: user must be logged in
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Ensure the Query input is focused
3. Do not type anything (leave Query empty) and wait the live_search debounce period
test_cases.expected_result: The results dropdown does not appear and no groups or 'No results found' message are visible; this behavior succeeds (Query == '' keeps dropdown hidden).

test_cases.test_case: Single-character Query shows dropdown with grouped results
preconditions: user must be logged in
Global Search opened
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter one character into the Query input
3. Wait for the live_search results to load
test_cases.expected_result: Search results dropdown appears grouped by entity type (Clients, Groups, Loans, Savings accounts) showing Entity_Name, Identifier, and Status where matches exist; the dropdown visibility and grouped results succeed for a single-character Query.

test_cases.test_case: Whitespace-only Query is treated as empty (dropdown remains hidden)
preconditions: user must be logged in
Global Search opened
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Enter only whitespace characters into the Query input (e.g., a single space)
3. Wait for the live_search debounce period
test_cases.expected_result: The system treats a whitespace-only Query as empty: the results dropdown does not appear and 'No results found' is not shown; this behavior succeeds (Query effectively treated as '').

test_cases.test_case: Rapid typing then immediate change yields results only for final Query (no stale selectable results)
preconditions: user must be logged in
Global Search opened
steps: 1. Click the Search Icon in the top toolbar to open Global Search
2. Rapidly type a short sequence of characters into Query (keystroke cadence faster than the live_search debounce)
3. Immediately after, use backspace/typing to change the Query to a different short final value
4. Wait for the live_search results to load for the final Query
5. Click a visible result item in the dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Only results corresponding to the final Query are displayed in the dropdown (no stale/intermediate results remain selectable), and clicking a visible result navigates to that item's detail page exactly once; the system correctly shows final-search results and navigation succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Search clients by Name returns only matching rows
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
At least one client exists with Name matching <search term> and other clients exist with different names
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Enter <search term> in the Client_Search field
3. Press Enter or click the search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Clients_Table displays only rows matching <search term> in Name/Account_No/External_ID/Mobile_Number; unrelated client rows are not visible

test_cases.test_case: Filter clients by Status shows only those status rows
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
Clients exist in multiple statuses including 'Active'
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Open the Status_Filter dropdown
3. Select 'Active' from the Status_Filter
test_cases.expected_result: Clients_Table displays only rows whose Status column shows 'Active' (green); rows with other status badges are not visible

test_cases.test_case: Open client from Clients table navigates to Client Detail page
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
A client exists with Name = <target client>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click the Name link for <target client> in the Clients_Table
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page is displayed for <target client>; the client's name is visible and the Status badge is shown on the page

test_cases.test_case: Create Client wizard happy path creates client in Pending status
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click the Create Client button to open the Create Client wizard
3. In Step 1 (General Details) select <Office> in Office
4. Enter <First name> in First_Name and <Last name> in Last_Name
5. Enter <Submitted date> in Submitted_On
6. (Optional) enter a unique value in External_ID as <unique external id>
7. Navigate through Step 2 and Step 3 using Next (no required fields)
8. In Step 4 (Identifiers) do not add identifiers for this test
9. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page is displayed for the new client; Status badge shows 'Pending' (yellow) and the client's name is visible on the page

test_cases.test_case: Create Client wizard with Identifiers adds identifier and results are visible on detail page
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click the Create Client button to open the Create Client wizard
3. In Step 1 (General Details) select <Office> and enter <First name>, <Last name>, and <Submitted date>
4. Proceed to Step 4 (Identifiers)
5. Click Add Row in the Create_Identifiers repeating group
6. Enter <Document Type A> in Document_Type and <Document Key A> in Document_Key for the new row
7. (Optional) Add a second identifier row with <Document Type B> and <Document Key B> to demonstrate multiple identifiers
8. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page is displayed for the new client; Status badge shows 'Pending' (yellow) and on the Identifiers tab the added identifier rows list the entered Document_Type and Document_Key values

test_cases.test_case: Bulk Import page Download Template triggers client Excel template download
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click Import Client to navigate to the Bulk Import page
3. Click the Download_Client_Template button
test_cases.expected_result: File download is triggered in the browser for the client Excel template

test_cases.test_case: Bulk Import upload queues import and creates an Import History row
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
A valid client import file <valid import file> is available
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click Import Client to navigate to the Bulk Import page
3. Click Upload_Client_File and select <valid import file> from the OS file dialog
4. Confirm the upload
test_cases.expected_result: A new row appears in the Import_History table for <valid import file>; the row is visible in the Import History table with Name corresponding to the uploaded file

test_cases.test_case: Download import result from Import History triggers file download
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
Import_History contains a completed import row for <completed import>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Clients page
2. Click Import Client to navigate to the Bulk Import page
3. In the Import_History table click the Download action for the row corresponding to <completed import>
test_cases.expected_result: File download is triggered in the browser for the import result or error file associated with <completed import>

test_cases.test_case: Activate action on Pending client sets status to Active and shows Activation Date
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
A client exists in Pending status with Submitted_On = <submitted date> and Client Detail page is open for that client
steps: 1. On the Client Detail page ensure the action bar shows Activate in Pending state
2. Click Activate
3. Enter <activation date> in Activation_Date (where <activation date> is on or after <submitted date>)
4. Click Confirm on the Activate dialog
test_cases.expected_result: On the Client Detail page the Status badge updates to 'Active' (green) and the Activation Date field displays <activation date>

test_cases.test_case: Reject action on Pending client sets status to Rejected
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
A client exists in Pending status and Client Detail page is open for that client
steps: 1. On the Client Detail page click Reject in the action bar
2. Enter <rejection reason> in Reason
3. Click Confirm on the Reject dialog
test_cases.expected_result: On the Client Detail page the Status badge updates to 'Rejected' (red)

test_cases.test_case: Transfer Client action on Active client updates Office to selected Destination_Office
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
A client exists in Active status with Office = <current office> and Client Detail page is open for that client
steps: 1. On the Client Detail page click Transfer Client in the action bar
2. Select <Destination_Office> (different from <current office>) in Destination_Office
3. Click Confirm on the Transfer dialog
test_cases.expected_result: On the Client Detail page the Office field updates to the selected <Destination_Office>

test_cases.test_case: Navigate across all tabs and Accounts sub-tabs on the Client Detail page
preconditions: User logged in as <Staff>
Client Detail page is open for a client
steps: 1. On the Client Detail page click the General tab and verify its panel is visible
2. Click the Accounts tab and verify the Accounts panel is visible
3. Within Accounts click the Loans sub-tab and verify its panel is visible
4. Click the Savings sub-tab and verify its panel is visible
5. Click the Shares sub-tab and verify its panel is visible
6. Click the Fixed Deposits sub-tab and verify its panel is visible
7. Click the Recurring Deposits sub-tab and verify its panel is visible
8. Click the Identifiers tab and verify the Identifiers panel with the Detail_Identifiers repeating group is visible
9. Click the Family Members tab and verify its panel is visible
10. Click the Notes tab and verify its panel is visible
11. Click the Documents tab and verify its panel is visible
test_cases.expected_result: Each selected tab/sub-tab displays its corresponding panel; Accounts shows sub-tabs Loans, Savings, Shares, Fixed Deposits and Recurring Deposits and Identifiers displays the identifiers repeating-group area

test_cases.test_case: Leave required text field (Office) blank in Create Client wizard
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Clients page
Create Client wizard is opened
steps: 1. In Step 1 (General Details) clear the Office field
2. Enter <valid First Name> into the First Name field
3. Enter <valid Last Name> into the Last Name field
4. Enter <valid date> into the Submitted On field
5. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Office field indicating it is required; the form does not submit and no client is created

test_cases.test_case: Center Detail: rapid consecutive state actions (Close then Activate) are serialized or blocked

User is on that center's Center_Detail_Page

2. Immediately click 'Activate' in the action bar (before the first action visibly completes)
test_cases.expected_result: The second action is blocked while the first action is in progress (action buttons become disabled or the second click has no effect). Only one state transition occurs at a time; the UI shows processing state and the center ends in the state corresponding to the single successfully completed action

test_cases.test_case: Select Accounting Method = Cash-based and leave required GL account mapping blank

test_cases.test_case: Tier Period From equals Period To is accepted

test_cases.test_case: Tier Period From greater than Period To is blocked

test_cases.test_case: FD: Deposit Amount exactly at product interest-rate tier lower bound picks tier rate
preconditions: A Fixed Deposit product exists with an Interest Rate Chart that has a tier breakpoint at <tier lower bound>
User is on the Client Detail page
steps: 1. Click 'Create Fixed Deposit Account' in the Client Detail Actions
2. Select the Fixed Deposit product that has a tier with lower bound <tier lower bound> in Fixed_Deposit_Product dropdown
3. Enter <tier lower bound> in the Deposit_Amount field
4. Enter a valid value in Deposit_Period_Value that corresponds to a term covered by the product chart
5. Select the appropriate Deposit_Period_Unit
6. Select a Maturity_Instructions option
7. Click the create_action button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; creation of the Fixed Deposit account succeeds; FD Account Detail page displays an interest rate equal to the rate defined for the tier that begins at <tier lower bound> and the maturity amount displayed is calculated using that tier rate

test_cases.test_case: FD: Deposit Amount one unit below a tier lower bound maps to the lower tier rate
preconditions: A Fixed Deposit product exists with an Interest Rate Chart that has a tier breakpoint at <tier lower bound>
User is on the Client Detail page
steps: 1. Click 'Create Fixed Deposit Account' in the Client Detail Actions
2. Select the Fixed Deposit product that has a tier with lower bound <tier lower bound> in Fixed_Deposit_Product dropdown
3. Enter <one unit below tier lower bound> in the Deposit_Amount field
4. Enter a valid value in Deposit_Period_Value that corresponds to a term covered by the product chart
5. Select the appropriate Deposit_Period_Unit
6. Select a Maturity_Instructions option
7. Click the create_action button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; creation of the Fixed Deposit account succeeds; FD Account Detail page displays an interest rate corresponding to the lower tier (i.e., the tier that applies to <one unit below tier lower bound>) — visible interest rate does not reflect the higher-tier rate

test_cases.test_case: FD: Deposit Period Value exactly at product term-tier lower bound selects that term tier rate

2. Select the Fixed Deposit product that has a term-tier lower bound at <term tier lower bound> in Fixed_Deposit_Product dropdown
3. Enter a Deposit_Amount that falls within the product's expected amounts
4. Enter <term tier lower bound> in Deposit_Period_Value
5. Select the Deposit_Period_Unit that matches the chart's unit
6. Select a Maturity_Instructions option
7. Click the create_action button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; creation of the Fixed Deposit account succeeds; FD Account Detail page displays an interest rate equal to the rate defined for the term tier that begins at <term tier lower bound> and the maturity amount is calculated accordingly

test_cases.test_case: FD: Deposit Period Value one unit below term-tier lower bound applies the previous term tier rate

User is on the Client Detail page
steps: 1. Click 'Create Fixed Deposit Account' in the Client Detail Actions

3. Enter a Deposit_Amount that falls within the product's expected amounts
4. Enter <one unit below term tier lower bound> in Deposit_Period_Value
5. Select the Deposit_Period_Unit that matches the chart's unit
6. Select a Maturity_Instructions option
7. Click the create_action button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; creation of the Fixed Deposit account succeeds; FD Account Detail page displays an interest rate corresponding to the prior term tier (i.e., the tier that applies to <one unit below term tier lower bound>) rather than the higher-tier rate

6. Enter <Account name> in the Account_Name field
7. Click the submit button

Submitting the form with Account_Usage = 'Detail' and no Parent_Account selected succeeds; a new Detail account appears in the Chart_of_Accounts_Tree under the chosen Account_Type with no parent shown (succeeds). The UI must show the new account row in the appropriate group.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of submit creates only one GL account
preconditions: User is on Chart_of_Accounts_Page
No existing account uses <unique GL code> to be entered
steps: 1. Click '+ Create GL Account' to open Create_GL_Account_Form
2. Select <Account_Type> in the Account_Type dropdown
3. Enter <unique GL code> in the GL_Code field
4. Enter <Account name> in the Account_Name field
5. Fill any other required fields
6. Rapidly double-click the submit button (two clicks within ~200ms)

test_cases.expected_result: Debit total and Credit total shown on the form are equal and the Difference displays 0; a new row appears in the Journal Entries table showing the entered Office, Transaction Date, and total Amount

steps: 1. In the Parameters form, select valid values for any required dropdowns or date fields (if applicable)

test_cases.test_case: Export the selected report to CSV from the Parameters form
preconditions: User logged in as <User with report access>

steps: 1. In the Parameters form, select valid values for any required dropdowns or date fields (if applicable)

test_cases.test_case: Export current generated report view to PDF from the Generated Report Table actions

steps: 1. In the Generated Report Table, click the 'Export to PDF' available action

test_cases.test_case: Verify sorting and pagination controls on the generated report table
preconditions: User logged in as <User with report access>
Reports page is open and a Generated_Report_Table is visible (report has been run with multiple pages of results)
steps: 1. Click a sortable column header in the Generated_Report_Table
2. Observe the table row order and sort indicator
3. Click the pagination control to go to the next page
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking a sortable column header shows a sort indicator and the table row order is updated accordingly. Using the pagination control moves to page 2 and the table displays a different set of rows; the pagination control shows the current page as 2.

test_cases.test_case: Run Report with invalid date format in Date_From
preconditions: Reports page is open
steps: 1. Click the 'All' tab
2. Click the report row 'Loans Awaiting Disbursal' to open the Parameters form
3. Enter <invalid/impossible date> in the Date_From field
4. Click the 'Run Report' button
test_cases.expected_result: Date_From field displays an inline validation error indicating the value is not a valid date; the Run Report action is blocked; Generated_Report_Table is not displayed and no export/download is initiated.

test_cases.test_case: Run Report with Date_From later than Date_To (reversed date range)
preconditions: Reports page is open
steps: 1. Click the 'All' tab
2. Click the report row 'Loans Awaiting Disbursal' to open the Parameters form
3. Enter <date later than Date_To> in the Date_From field
4. Enter <earlier date> in the Date_To field
5. Click the 'Run Report' button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline cross-field validation error appears on the Date_From and/or Date_To fields indicating the start date must be before the end date; the Run Report action is blocked; Generated_Report_Table is not displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Export to Excel from Parameters form without running report
preconditions: Reports page is open
steps: 1. Click the 'All' tab
2. Click the report row 'Active Loans Summary' to open the Parameters form
3. Click the 'Export to Excel' button in the Parameters form without clicking 'Run Report'
test_cases.expected_result: No Excel file is downloaded; the export action is blocked; a visible message or banner on the Parameters form indicates exporting requires a generated report (for example: 'Run the report before exporting'); Generated_Report_Table is not displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt export from Generated_Report_Table when report returns no rows
preconditions: Reports page is open
steps: 1. Click the 'All' tab
2. Click the report row 'Active Loans Details' to open the Parameters form
3. Enter <date with no data> in the Date_From field
4. Enter <date with no data> in the Date_To field
5. Click the 'Run Report' button
6. Confirm Generated_Report_Table displays no rows / shows a 'no results' state
7. Click the 'Export to CSV' action in the Generated_Report_Table
test_cases.expected_result: Export is blocked (Export button disabled or an inline/global message shown); no CSV file is downloaded; a visible message near the table indicates there is no data to export and export controls are disabled or show an error.

test_cases.test_case: Date_From equals Date_To (same-day) is accepted
preconditions: User is on the Reports page
A report row is available (e.g., 'Loans Awaiting Disbursal')
steps: 1. Click the report row's 'Open Parameters' action to open Parameters_Form
2. Enter <same date> in Date_From
3. Enter <same date> in Date_To
4. Click the 'Run Report' button
test_cases.expected_result: Run Report succeeds; Generated_Report_Table is displayed with sorting and pagination controls and no field-level date validation error is shown for Date_From/Date_To

test_cases.test_case: Date_From one day after Date_To is blocked
preconditions: User is on the Reports page
A report row is available
steps: 1. Click the report row's 'Open Parameters' action to open Parameters_Form
2. Enter <one day after <Date_To>> in Date_From
3. Enter <selected date> in Date_To
4. Click the 'Run Report' button
test_cases.expected_result: Run Report is blocked; an inline validation error is shown on Date_From or Date_To indicating the start date cannot be after the end date (visible next to the date field) and no Generated_Report_Table is shown

test_cases.test_case: Far-future Date_From is accepted or handled without validation error
preconditions: User is on the Reports page
A report row is available
steps: 1. Click the report row's 'Open Parameters' action to open Parameters_Form
2. Enter <far future date> in Date_From
3. Optionally enter <far future date> or leave Date_To blank
4. Click the 'Run Report' button
test_cases.expected_result: Run Report succeeds; Generated_Report_Table is displayed (may be empty) and no date validation error is shown for the far-future date

test_cases.test_case: Run Report with both Date_From and Date_To left blank
preconditions: User is on the Reports page
A report row is available
steps: 1. Click the report row's 'Open Parameters' action to open Parameters_Form
2. Leave Date_From blank
3. Leave Date_To blank
4. Click the 'Run Report' button
test_cases.expected_result: Run Report succeeds; Generated_Report_Table is displayed (may contain full result set or be empty) and no date-related validation error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click on 'Run Report' should not produce duplicate tables or duplicate processing
preconditions: User is on the Reports page
Parameters_Form is open for a selected report
steps: 1. Ensure Date_From/Date_To (or other parameters) are set or left blank per test intent
2. Double-click the 'Run Report' button quickly (two clicks within a short interval)
test_cases.expected_result: Only one Run Report action is processed; a single Generated_Report_Table appears with sorting and pagination controls (no duplicate tables or duplicate result blocks are shown) and any second click is ignored or the button becomes disabled while processing

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click on 'Export to Excel' should initiate only one download
preconditions: User has successfully run a report and Generated_Report_Table is visible
steps: 1. Click or focus the Generated_Report_Table's 'Export to Excel' action
2. Double-click the 'Export to Excel' action quickly (two clicks within a short interval)
test_cases.expected_result: Only one Excel download is initiated; the UI shows a single download action (the second click is ignored or the export button is disabled) and no duplicate download prompts appear

test_cases.test_case: Pressing browser Back immediately after running report returns to blank Parameters_Form and does not auto-create duplicates
preconditions: User is on the Reports page
Parameters_Form is open and Run Report has been clicked successfully resulting in Generated_Report_Table
steps: 1. After Generated_Report_Table is displayed, press the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: Browser Back returns the user to the Parameters_Form and the form fields are blank (no residual selections); no additional Generated_Report_Table is automatically created by navigating back and there is no duplicate report processing triggered

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to Enable a standing instruction that is already Active (wrong-state action)

test_cases.test_case: Transfer amount exactly equals available balance (allowed)
preconditions: User is on the Account Transfers form
A source account is selected that has an available balance of <available balance>
A destination account is selected
steps: 1. Enter <available balance> in the Transfer_Amount field
2. Enter a valid date in the Transfer_Date field
3. Fill any other required/visible fields
4. Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; transfer processing succeeds: the transfer is processed, the source account balance is reduced by the entered amount, no 'exceeds available balance' error is shown, and a visible success confirmation appears.

test_cases.test_case: Transfer amount one unit above available balance (blocked)
preconditions: User is on the Account Transfers form
A source account is selected that has an available balance of <available balance>
A destination account is selected
steps: 1. Enter <one unit above available balance> in the Transfer_Amount field
2. Enter a valid date in the Transfer_Date field
3. Fill any other required/visible fields
4. Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked / error shown: the Transfer_Amount field displays an inline error indicating the amount exceeds the available balance and the transfer is not processed.

test_cases.test_case: Transfer Date = today
preconditions: User is on the Account Transfers form
A source account exists with sufficient available balance for the chosen amount
A destination account is selected
steps: 1. Enter today in the Transfer_Date field
2. Enter a valid Transfer_Amount less than the available balance
3. Fill any other required/visible fields
4. Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; transfer is scheduled/processed for today's date and a visible confirmation or transaction entry shows the chosen Transfer_Date as today.

test_cases.test_case: Transfer Date set far in the future
preconditions: User is on the Account Transfers form
A source account exists with sufficient available balance
A destination account is selected
steps: 1. Enter a date far in the future (e.g., many years from today) in the Transfer_Date field
2. Enter a valid Transfer_Amount less than the available balance
3. Fill any other required/visible fields
4. Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission succeeds and the transfer is accepted/scheduled for the specified future date; the transfer detail or confirmation displays the entered future Transfer_Date.

test_cases.test_case: Transfer_Amount with excessive decimal precision
preconditions: User is on the Account Transfers form
A source account exists with sufficient available balance
A destination account is selected
steps: 1. Enter a Transfer_Amount value containing many decimal places (more precision than typical currency precision) in the Transfer_Amount field
2. Enter a valid Transfer_Date
3. Fill any other required/visible fields
4. Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: If the system accepts extra precision, the form submits successfully and the saved transfer detail displays the amount rounded/truncated to the system's stored precision; if the system does not accept it, the Transfer_Amount field displays an inline validation error blocking submission.

test_cases.test_case: Very long Name in Create Standing Instruction
preconditions: User is on the Standing Instructions page
User clicks '+ Create Standing Instruction' to open the Create_Standing_Instruction_Form
steps: 1. Enter a very long string (>200 characters) in the Name field
2. Fill the minimal required fields to allow creation (if any) or leave optional fields blank
3. Click the Create button
test_cases.expected_result: If accepted, the standing instruction is created and appears in the listing; the listing may display the Name truncated (ellipsised) while the detail view shows the full Name. If not accepted, the Name field displays a visible validation error indicating the length limit and creation is blocked.

test_cases.test_case: Double-click Submit on Account Transfers (prevent duplicates)
preconditions: User is on the Account Transfers form with all required fields filled and a source account with sufficient available balance
No transfer for these exact inputs exists immediately prior to test
steps: 1. Perform a rapid double-click (two quick clicks) on the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Only one transfer is processed: a single success confirmation is shown and only one transaction/transfer record appears. No duplicate transfer record is created.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid Enable then Disable of a standing instruction row
preconditions: User is on the Standing Instructions page
A standing instruction exists in the listing with Status = Disabled
steps: 1. Click the Enable action on the standing instruction row
2. Immediately click the Disable action on the same row before the first action fully completes
3. Observe the row Status in the listing
test_cases.expected_result: Actions succeed without creating inconsistent state: the final Status displays as Disabled, no duplicate rows are created, and no persistent error is shown. The rapid consecutive state transitions complete with a consistent final state.

test_cases.expected_result: If the system limits decimal precision, an inline validation error is displayed or the value is rounded to the supported precision and the form succeeds; the Tax Component detail shows the rounded saved value (succeeds or error shown). If no precision limit, the form submits successfully and the exact value is displayed (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Double-click submit on Create Tax Component form

steps: 1. Click the submit button twice rapidly (double-click)

test_cases.test_case: Add then remove all Tax Component entries in Tax Group repeating group before submit
preconditions: User is on Tax Groups page
Create Tax Group form is opened
steps: 1. Click to add a Tax Components repeating-group item
2. Select a Tax Component in the new repeating-group item

4. Click to add a second repeating-group item
5. Select a Tax Component and enter dates for the second item
6. Remove the first repeating-group item
7. Remove the second repeating-group item (so no items remain)
8. Enter a valid Name in the form
9. Click the submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Because the AST does not define a minimum required count for Tax Components, submission succeeds and a Tax Group is created with zero associated components; the Tax Groups table shows the new group with no associated components (succeeds). If the UI enforces at least one component, an inline error appears indicating at least one component is required and submission is blocked (error shown).

test_cases.test_case: Start Date entered far in the future for Tax Component

test_cases.expected_result: If the system enforces a not-in-future constraint for Start Date, an inline error is shown and submission is blocked (error shown). Otherwise, the form submits successfully and the Tax Component detail/list shows the entered future Start Date (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Log out via Profile dropdown redirects to Login page
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the <User Profile> icon in the top-right corner to open the dropdown
2. Click the 'Log Out' button in the dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Login page and the Login form is visible

test_cases.test_case: After logout, attempt to open an authenticated page redirects to Login page
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the <User Profile> icon in the top-right corner to open the dropdown
2. Click the 'Log Out' button in the dropdown
3. Attempt to navigate to an authenticated page by entering the <authenticated route> in the browser address bar or clicking a bookmark to an authenticated page
test_cases.expected_result: Attempt to access the authenticated page is intercepted and the user is redirected to the Login page; the Login form is visible

test_cases.test_case: Profile dropdown toggles closed when Profile icon clicked again
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
steps: 1. Click the <User Profile> icon in the top-right corner to open the dropdown
2. Click the <User Profile> icon again
test_cases.expected_result: Profile dropdown is no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user tries to access an authenticated route
preconditions: User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged out / not authenticated
2. In the browser address bar, navigate to <authenticated page URL>
test_cases.expected_result: The Authenticated_Route_Guard triggers: the browser is redirected to the login page; the authenticated page content is not displayed and the URL changes to the login page (i.e., access is blocked)

test_cases.test_case: Profile icon and Log Out are not available when user is logged out
preconditions: User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged out / not authenticated
2. Open the application landing page / main UI
test_cases.expected_result: User_Profile_Icon is not visible on the page; Profile_Dropdown is not accessible and the 'Log Out' item is not present (user cannot access logout when not logged in)

test_cases.test_case: Direct attempt to invoke logout action while not authenticated is blocked
preconditions: User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged out / not authenticated
2. In the browser address bar, navigate directly to <logout action URL> and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: The logout precondition ('user must be logged in') fails and the request is blocked: the browser is redirected to the login page; no logout flow is performed and no authenticated session is terminated (i.e., logout action cannot be executed by unauthenticated user)

test_cases.test_case: After logging out, navigating back to a protected page is blocked
preconditions: User is authenticated at test start
steps: 1. Log in as a valid user and verify authenticated UI is shown
2. Click User_Profile_Icon
3. Click 'Log Out' in the Profile_Dropdown
4. After logout completes and the login page is shown, use the browser Back button or navigate to <previous authenticated page URL>
test_cases.expected_result: Authenticated route guard redirects the browser to the login page; the previously authenticated page content is not restored and no authenticated content is visible (access after logout is blocked)

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of 'Log Out' button
preconditions: user is logged in
User_Profile_Icon is visible
Profile_Dropdown is visible after clicking User_Profile_Icon
steps: 1. Click User_Profile_Icon to open Profile_Dropdown
2. Rapidly double-click the 'Log Out' button (two clicks within 300ms)
test_cases.expected_result: Single logout action succeeds: the app performs one session termination and redirects to the login page once; authentication token is cleared; no error message is shown; User_Profile_Icon and Profile_Dropdown are not visible after redirect.

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Log Out' then immediately click 'Profile Settings' in the dropdown
preconditions: user is logged in
User_Profile_Icon is visible
Profile_Dropdown is visible after clicking User_Profile_Icon
steps: 1. Click User_Profile_Icon to open Profile_Dropdown
2. Click 'Log Out' once
3. Immediately (before redirect finishes) click 'Profile Settings' in the same Profile_Dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to Profile Settings is blocked; the logout action still succeeds and the app redirects to the login page; Profile Settings page is not displayed and the Profile_Dropdown is not accessible after redirect.

test_cases.test_case: Use browser Back after logout to reach an authenticated page
preconditions: user is logged in
User_Profile_Icon is visible
steps: 1. Click User_Profile_Icon to open Profile_Dropdown
2. Click 'Log Out' once and confirm redirect to login page
3. Press the browser Back button to navigate to the previously viewed authenticated page
test_cases.expected_result: Attempt to navigate back to an authenticated page is blocked by the Authenticated_Route_Guard; the user is redirected to the login page; authentication token remains cleared; User_Profile_Icon is not visible on the resulting page.
